text.title: LB Technology Selection Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for LB technology selection. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: On AWS / GCP / Azure, the managed LB (ALB / NLB / Global LB / Cloud LB / App Gateway / Front Door) MUST be the default choice. Self-hosted HAProxy / Nginx on cloud VMs is allowed only with a documented reason (compliance, plugin requirement, cost).
rule.rationale: Managed LBs deliver HA, cert rotation, anycast, and DDoS protection out of the box. Self-hosting reinvents the wheel and adds 1+ FTE of ops burden.

rule.statement: For Kubernetes workloads, the LB MUST be a Kubernetes Ingress controller (or Gateway API), not a raw HAProxy / Nginx outside the cluster targeting node IPs.
rule.rationale: An Ingress controller registers backend pods automatically via Endpoints; an external LB hard-coded to node IPs breaks every time the cluster scales.

rule.statement: For bare-metal deployments (no managed cloud LB and no Kubernetes), the LB MUST be HAProxy (TCP-heavy + rate limiting) or Nginx (HTTP-only + static caching). MetalLB + ingress-nginx is allowed for bare-metal K8s.
rule.rationale: These two cover 95 % of bare-metal use cases; using bespoke C/Go LBs on bare-metal adds maintenance debt with no upside.

rule.statement: The chosen technology MUST be capable of the layer chosen in lb-layer-selection. Pure L4: NLB / HAProxy mode tcp. L7: ALB / Nginx / HAProxy mode http / Envoy. Hybrid: L4 in front of L7.
rule.rationale: Layer + tech inconsistency (e.g., "ALB for raw TCP") is a top architecture-review failure; tie the decision back to lb-layer-selection explicitly.

rule.statement: Self-hosted HAProxy / Nginx LBs require ≥ 2 engineers with on-call coverage trained on the tool. Teams below that capacity MUST default to managed LBs even at higher cost.
rule.rationale: A single owner = SPOF on the operations side; 2 a.m. cert rotation failures need backup. Managed LBs let small teams ship without staffing for LB ops.
